content: I understand. To calibrate the initial phase-lock frequency when transitioning from propofol to dexmedetomidine in a bidirectional sedation equilibrium protocol, you typically start by matching the sedation depth and EEG spectral characteristics observed under propofol, then adjust the dexmedetomidine infusion rate to achieve a stable phase-lock frequency that maintains equivalent sedation levels, often guided by continuous EEG monitoring and sedation scales.
